questionText: A firework is launched so that, $t$ seconds after launch, its position in metres is modelled by $x = 6t$ and $y = 24t - 4t^{2}$. Find the greatest height the firework reaches, and how far it has travelled horizontally at that moment.

steps.description: Differentiate the height with respect to t
steps.explanation: The vertical velocity $\dot{y}$ tells us how fast the firework is rising, found by differentiating the height equation.

steps.description: State the condition for greatest height
steps.explanation: At the top of the flight the firework is momentarily neither rising nor falling, so its vertical velocity is zero.
Setting $\dot{y}$ equal to zero gives an equation for the time at which the highest point occurs.
Rearranging gives the time, in seconds, at which the firework reaches its greatest height.
Substituting the time back into the height equation gives the actual greatest height reached.

steps.description: Find the horizontal distance at that time
steps.explanation: Using the same time in the $x$-equation gives how far the firework has travelled horizontally by then.
So the greatest height is $36$ m, reached when the firework has travelled $18$ m horizontally.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The greatest height is $36$ m, reached when the firework has travelled $18$ m horizontally (at $t = 3$ s).
